description_long: This is a vintage CRT television with a boxy, rectangular form and a prominent convex glass screen. The front panel features a set of rotary dials and push buttons on the lower right, along with a brand label reading 'SAMP 4'. The casing is primarily a muted beige color with a darker gray rear section, which houses the bulky cathode ray tube and ventilation grilles. The back of the television is solid and features additional venting and two visible port holes, likely for power and antenna connections. The sides are plain except for a circular vent on the right and some subtle surface wear, indicating age and use. The overall design is utilitarian, with hard plastic and metal construction typical of late 20th-century consumer electronics.
description: A vintage beige and gray CRT television with a convex glass screen, rotary dials, and a bulky rear housing. The front features control knobs and a 'SAMP 4' label.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, rotary dials, push buttons, and 'SAMP 4' branding.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain beige side panel and the depth of the television's rear housing.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the dark gray back panel, ventilation grilles, and two port holes.
description_view_3: Right side view with a circular vent and visible surface wear on the beige panel.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick beige plastic bezel with a matte finish and squared edges.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and push buttons on the lower right of the front panel.
